title: Notopleura lateralis
taxonomy: Notopleura lateralis is a species within the family Rubiaceae. This classification places it within the order Gentianales. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, it is closely related to other plants such as coffee and gardenias. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, comprising over 600 genera. The classification of Notopleura lateralis is supported by its shared characteristics and molecular analysis.
uses: Notopleura lateralis is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon rainforest region. Its extracts and compounds have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various medicinal uses, including treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications. The traditional uses of Notopleura lateralis may offer valuable insights into the development of new treatments for various diseases.

intro: Notopleura lateralis, also known as Quina, is a medicinal plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Notopleura lateralis include alkaloids, such as quinamine and quinine, as well as phenolic compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into remedies like cough syrups and expectorants.
